title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Browser and… · Inference Brief: Transport Abstraction
summary: A published TypeScript SDK needs typed operation boundaries, an injected fetch function, ordered wrappers, runtime validators, and environment-safe configuration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts transport abstraction increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
